items.definition_en: Baking powder is a white powder used to make baked goods rise.
items.example_en: She added some baking powder to the cake batter.

items.definition_en: A marinade is a liquid mixture used to flavor and soften food before cooking.
items.example_en: The chicken tasted great after soaking in the marinade.

items.definition_en: A utensil is a tool used for cooking or preparing food.
items.example_en: Every kitchen needs at least a few basic utensils.

items.definition_en: Stock is a flavored liquid made by cooking meat, fish, or vegetables in water.
items.example_en: She used homemade chicken stock for the risotto.

items.definition_en: A glaze is a shiny coating added to food for taste or appearance.
items.example_en: He brushed a sweet glaze over the ham.

items.definition_en: An infusion is a liquid created by soaking an ingredient in hot water.
items.example_en: They made a herbal infusion to serve with dessert.

items.definition_en: A confection is a sweet food such as candy or cake.
items.example_en: She learned how to create an elegant confection in pastry class.

items.definition_en: Zest is the colorful skin of citrus fruit used to add flavor.
items.example_en: Add a little lemon zest to make the dish brighter.

items.definition_en: A fillet is a boneless piece of meat or fish.
items.example_en: I ordered a fillet of salmon for dinner.

items.definition_en: Caramelization is the process of browning sugar during cooking.
items.example_en: The onions gained sweetness through caramelization.
